# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud compute security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: compute-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    serviceAccounts:
    - email: custom-service-account@PROJECT_ID.iam.gserviceaccount.com
      scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-11
        diskType: zones/us-central1-a/diskTypes/pd-standard
        diskSizeGb: 10
        diskEncryptionKey:
          kmsKeyName: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/us-central1/keyRings/KEYRING_NAME/cryptoKeys/KEY_NAME
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
        networkTier: PREMIUM
    metadata:
      items:
      - key: block-project-ssh-keys
        value: "true"
      - key: enable-oslogin
        value: "true"
      - key: enable-guest-attributes
        value: "false"
    shieldedInstanceConfig:
      enableSecureBoot: true
      enableVtpm: true
      enableIntegrityMonitoring: true
    tags:
      items:
      - secure-instance
